summary.goal: Fix UTF-8 panic in search highlight() by replacing byte-index slicing with char-level iteration (closes #23)
decisions: Replaced byte-index slicing with char-vec iteration: collect text and lower as Vec<char>, search by char-slice equality, push chars directly — no byte offsets from lower ever used to index text
When char count diverges (rare: folding expansion like İ→i+combining-dot), return text unmodified — no highlight but no panic, acceptable for the edge case

rejected_approaches.description: Full char-tracking with lower_src map to handle mid-expansion queries
rejected_approaches.reason: Correct for all cases but substantially more complex; the simpler char-vec approach handles the described panic and all realistic inputs

open_threads: highlight() now allocates two Vec<char>; if perf ever matters for large node sets, a char-indices iterator approach avoids the allocations
